name: Rhinoceros
summary: White rhinoceroses are massive African grazers with two horns and wide mouths for eating grass. They live on open savannas and are among the largest land...
metaTitle: Rhinoceros Facts | Savanna Habitat, Horns & Photos
metaDescription: Discover white rhinoceros facts with photos, savanna habitat, grass diet, calf life, and conservation. Learn where rhinos live and why horns put them at risk.
searchIntents: what are rhino horns made of

lifespan.captivity: Up to 50 years in human care

speed: Up to 30 mph in short bursts
funFacts: White rhinos are not white—the name may come from the Afrikaans word for wide.
Rhino horns are made of keratin, like human fingernails.
White rhinos have wide, square lips perfect for grazing grass.
A white rhino can eat more than 100 pounds of grass in a day.
Rhinos love mud baths that cool their skin and discourage insects.
Despite their bulk, rhinos can run surprisingly fast.

faq.question: What is a rhinoceros?
faq.answer: A rhinoceros is a huge plant-eating mammal with thick skin and one or two horns on its nose.

faq.question: Where do white rhinos live?
faq.answer: Southern white rhinos live in grasslands and savannas of southern and eastern Africa.

faq.question: What do rhinos eat?
faq.answer: White rhinos are herbivores that graze on grasses and other low plants.

faq.question: Are rhinos endangered?
faq.answer: White rhinos are listed as Near Threatened. Poaching for horns remains a serious threat.

faq.question: What are rhino horns made of?
faq.answer: Rhino horns are made of keratin, the same protein found in human hair and fingernails.

faq.question: Why are they called white rhinos?
faq.answer: The name likely comes from the Afrikaans word wyd, meaning wide, referring to their broad grazing lips.

faq.question: What is a baby rhino called?
faq.answer: A baby rhinoceros is called a calf. Calves stay close to their mothers for protection.
